specification.topic-statement: The Google streaming event processor translates Gemini SSE response parts into normalized stream events, handling text, thinking, function calls, tool ID generation, thought signature extraction, and finish reason mapping.

scope.in-scope: Part-type dispatch (text, thinking, function call), random tool call ID generation, finish reason string mapping, thought signature extraction, usage metadata extraction, and error status mapping.
scope.boundary: How messages are serialized into the Google request format is covered by the provider serialization specification.
Raw SSE byte splitting and event framing are covered by the SSE parser specification. This topic processes already-parsed JSON event objects.

behavior.tool-id-generation:
Google's API does not provide unique identifiers for tool calls. A 22-character random identifier is generated for each tool call using the alphabet: ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_
The random number generator is seeded with the current time once per process (on the first tool call). Subsequent IDs are generated without re-seeding.
IDs are generated with rand() seeded once at process startup. They are not cryptographically random and are not guaranteed to be unique across processes or across a large number of calls.
behavior.finish-reason-mapping: Google finishReason strings are mapped to internal finish reasons: - "STOP" → STOP - "MAX_TOKENS" → LENGTH - "SAFETY", "BLOCKLIST", "PROHIBITED_CONTENT", "IMAGE_SAFETY", "IMAGE_PROHIBITED_CONTENT", "RECITATION" → CONTENT_FILTER - "MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL", "UNEXPECTED_TOOL_CALL" → ERROR - All other values (including absent) → UNKNOWN
behavior.thought-signature-extraction:
The thought signature is a provider-opaque value that accompanies Gemini 3 function call results. It is searched for in two locations in the response JSON: 1. Directly on the response root object. 2. Within candidates[0].
If found in either location, the signature is serialized as a JSON object with key "thought_signature" and returned as a null-terminated string. If not found or the value is an empty string, null is returned.
For performance, the signature search is only performed when the model uses the Gemini 3 series.
behavior.parts-iteration:
Each streaming chunk contains a parts array within the response content. Parts are processed in order:
A part with a "thought" field set to true is a thinking part. Any open tool call is ended before processing. The thinking flag is set. The part's text content is emitted as a THINKING_DELTA event.
A part with a text field and no "thought" flag is a text part. Any open tool call is ended before processing. If the previous part was a thinking part, the part index is incremented before emission. The text is emitted as a TEXT_DELTA event.
A part with a functionCall field is a tool invocation. If no tool call is currently open, a new tool call ID is generated, the function name is extracted, and a TOOL_CALL_START event is emitted. The function arguments are serialized from the parsed JSON object back to a string and emitted as a TOOL_CALL_DELTA event. The accumulated arguments string is updated by concatenation.
On Gemini 3 models, a thoughtSignature field adjacent to the functionCall in the part is also extracted and stored alongside the tool call state.
behavior.usage-metadata:
The final streaming chunk includes a usageMetadata object. The following fields are extracted: - promptTokenCount → input tokens - candidatesTokenCount → raw output tokens - thoughtsTokenCount → thinking tokens
Output tokens are calculated as candidatesTokenCount minus thoughtsTokenCount (because thinking tokens are counted separately). The cachedContentTokenCount field is not used; cached token count is always reported as zero.
After extracting usage, any open tool call is ended, and a DONE event is emitted with the finish reason and usage counts.
behavior.error-status-mapping:
In-stream error events contain a status string. These are mapped to error categories: - "UNAUTHENTICATED" → AUTH - "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" → RATE_LIMIT - "INVALID_ARGUMENT" → INVALID_ARG - All other values → UNKNOWN
The error message and category are emitted as an ERROR stream event.
